<objective>
Scaffold the Electron project with electron-vite, install Phase 1 dependencies, define all TypeScript interfaces and Zod schemas for game data, implement the DataCache file I/O utility, and configure Vitest with initial tests.

Purpose: Every subsequent plan depends on these types, cache utilities, and the test framework. This is the foundation layer.
Output: Running Electron app shell, typed data contracts, tested cache utilities, green test suite.
</objective>

<tasks>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Scaffold Electron project and install dependencies</name>
  <files>package.json, electron.vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, tsconfig.node.json, tsconfig.web.json, vitest.config.ts, src/main/index.ts, src/preload/preload.ts, src/renderer/index.html, src/renderer/src/App.tsx, src/renderer/src/main.tsx</files>
  <action>
NOTE: This task creates 11 files, but most are auto-generated by the electron-vite scaffold CLI. Only vitest.config.ts and minor edits to index.ts/App.tsx are hand-written.

Scaffold a new Electron + React + TypeScript project using electron-vite. Run:

```bash
npm create @electron-vite@latest tft-helper -- --template react-ts
```

If the scaffold creates files in a subdirectory, move them to the project root. Then install Phase 1 dependencies:

```bash
npm install axios cheerio zod electron-store
npm install -D vitest @types/node
```

Create `vitest.config.ts` at project root:
```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';
import { resolve } from 'path';

export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    globals: true,
    environment: 'node',
    include: ['src/**/*.test.ts'],
    alias: {
      '@main': resolve(__dirname, 'src/main'),
    },
  },
});
```

Update `src/main/index.ts` to be a minimal Electron app entry that creates a BrowserWindow and loads the renderer. Keep the default scaffold behavior but ensure the window title is "TFT Helper".

Update `src/preload/preload.ts` to expose a minimal contextBridge API:
```typescript
import { contextBridge } from 'electron';
contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('api', {});
```

Update `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` to show a simple placeholder: `<div>TFT Helper - Loading...</div>`.

Verify the app launches with `npx electron-vite dev` (run briefly, confirm window appears, then close).
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>npx electron-vite build 2>&1 | tail -5</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>Electron app builds successfully. Package.json has axios, cheerio, zod, electron-store as dependencies and vitest as devDependency. vitest.config.ts exists.</done>
</task>

<task type="auto" tdd="true">
  <name>Task 2: Define TypeScript types, Zod schemas, and DataCache utility</name>
  <files>src/main/data/types.ts, src/main/data/types.test.ts, src/main/data/DataCache.ts, src/main/data/DataCache.test.ts, src/main/game/types.ts</files>
  <behavior>
    - Zod ChampionSchema parses valid champion object { apiName, name, cost, traits, icon }
    - Zod ChampionSchema rejects object missing required field (e.g., no name)
    - Zod TraitSchema parses valid trait with effects array
    - Zod ItemSchema parses valid item with optional `from` array
    - Zod AugmentSchema parses valid augment with optional tier
    - Zod MetaCompSchema parses valid meta comp { name, units, traits, avgPlace, top4Rate, winRate, playRate }
    - Zod MetaCompSchema accepts optional itemPriorities (string[]) and positioning (object)
    - writeJsonFile creates file and parent directories, readJsonFile reads it back
    - readJsonFile returns null for non-existent file (no throw)
    - getStaticDataPath returns correct AppData path pattern
    - getMetaCachePath returns correct AppData path pattern
  </behavior>
  <action>
Create `src/main/data/types.ts` with Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types:

- `ChampionSchema`: apiName (string), name (string), cost (number), traits (string[]), icon (string), ability (optional object with name, desc, icon)
- `TraitSchema`: apiName (string), name (string), desc (string), icon (string), effects (array of { minUnits: number, style: number })
- `ItemSchema`: id (number), name (string), desc (string), icon (string), from (optional number[]), composition (optional number[])
- `AugmentSchema`: apiName (string), name (string), desc (string), icon (string), tier (optional number 1-3)
- `MetaCompSchema`: name (string), units (array of { apiName: string, items: string[], star: number optional }), traits (string[]), avgPlace (number), top4Rate (number), winRate (number), playRate (number), tier (optional string like "S", "A", "B"), itemPriorities (optional string[] — ordered list of preferred items for the comp's carry), positioning (optional object with z.record(z.string(), z.object({ row: z.number(), col: z.number() })) — maps unit apiName to board position)
- `AppConfigSchema`: patchVersion (string), userLocale (string default "en_us"), userRegion (string default "NA1"), metaScrapedAt (number default 0), metaScrapedPatch (string default "")

Per user decision: "Extract ALL available data: comps, winrates, pickrates, augment tiers, item priorities, positioning." The MetaCompSchema includes itemPriorities and positioning as optional fields since availability depends on the scrape source. The scraper (Plan 01-03) will populate these when data is available.

Export all schemas and their inferred types (e.g., `export type Champion = z.infer<typeof ChampionSchema>`).

Create `src/main/game/types.ts` with TypeScript interfaces (no Zod needed here since Live Client API data is transient):
- `LiveClientResponse`: gameData (object with gameMode string, gameTime number), allPlayers (array), activePlayer (object) -- keep loose for now, Phase 2 refines
- `GameState`: isGameActive (boolean), gameMode (string | null), lastCheckedAt (number)

Create `src/main/data/DataCache.ts`:
- `getStaticDataPath(set: string, locale: string): string` -- returns `path.join(app.getPath('userData'), 'static', set, locale + '.json')`
- `getMetaCachePath(): string` -- returns `path.join(app.getPath('userData'), 'meta', 'meta_cache.json')`
- `getImageCachePath(set: string): string` -- returns `path.join(app.getPath('userData'), 'static', set, 'images')`
- `writeJsonFile(filePath: string, data: unknown): Promise<void>` -- creates parent dirs with `fs.mkdir(recursive: true)`, writes with `fs.writeFile`
- `readJsonFile<T>(filePath: string): Promise<T | null>` -- returns parsed JSON or null if file doesn't exist (catch ENOENT)

For tests: In DataCache.test.ts, use a temp directory (vitest `beforeEach`/`afterEach` with `fs.mkdtemp`) instead of the real AppData path. Mock `app.getPath('userData')` to return the temp dir.

Write tests FIRST (RED), then implement (GREEN).
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>npx vitest run src/main/data/types.test.ts src/main/data/DataCache.test.ts</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>All Zod schemas validate correct data and reject invalid shapes. MetaCompSchema accepts optional itemPriorities and positioning fields. DataCache writeJsonFile/readJsonFile roundtrips successfully. readJsonFile returns null for missing files. Game types file exports LiveClientResponse and GameState interfaces. All tests pass.</done>
</task>
